qa_pairs.question: What was the operational status and eventual fate of the Niagara and Mist clusters in 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: Beginning January 6, 2025, the Niagara cluster operated at approximately 50% capacity and Mist at approximately 35% capacity to support system improvements and the transition to the new Trillium system. Both clusters underwent staged reductions in September 2025, with Mist being decommissioned on September 16, and Niagara fully decommissioned on September 30, 2025.

qa_pairs.question: What was the status of the Graham compute cluster during its reduction and before its retirement in 2025?
qa_pairs.answer: From February 25, 2025, the Graham compute cluster operated at approximately 25% capacity to facilitate data migration and system transition work, leading up to its full decommissioning on September 1, 2025. During this period, user logins remained available, storage was accessible (though project storage was temporarily read-only), and Graham Cloud remained fully operational.

qa_pairs.question: What services and systems were impacted by the tape storage system migration from July 15 to September 8, 2025, for Béluga, Narval, and Juno?
qa_pairs.answer: From July 15 to September 8, 2025, file backup and restore services, as well as the TSM service itself, were fully unavailable due to a tape storage system migration. Files on Béluga and Narval in /nearline that had been migrated to tape were inaccessible. Data created or modified between July 15 and August 11 could not be recovered.
